evals.prompt: I have a skill at tests/fixtures/sample-skill/ that's not performing well. Run autoresearch on it to improve it. Use 2 iterations max.
evals.expected_output: Creates a workspace, snapshots the skill, attempts to run the improvement loop with evaluation and keep/discard logic

deterministic_checks.description: Transcript file was created in the outputs directory
Transcript contains evidence of mkdir execution (workspace creation)
Transcript references the snapshot operation
Transcript references the v0 baseline directory
Transcript references the candidate working copy
Transcript references results logging
Transcript references the evals file for grading
Transcript contains a numeric score or pass rate from evaluation
Transcript contains keep/discard decision language

expectations: Creates a workspace directory named sample-skill-autoresearch as a sibling to the skill
Snapshots the original skill to a v0 directory in the workspace
Creates a candidate copy of the skill in the workspace
Runs the evaluation step with actual tool calls (Bash commands visible in transcript), not just descriptions of what would happen
Checks for evals/evals.json in the skill directory
The keep/discard decision is based on comparing the new score against the best score

evals.prompt: Run autoresearch in eval-doctor mode on tests/fixtures/sample-skill/ — it needs proper evaluation cases created.
evals.expected_output: Detects eval-doctor mode, analyzes the sample skill, and creates evals/evals.json with well-structured evaluation cases

deterministic_checks.description: Output evals.json file was created
Output evals.json is valid JSON
evals.json has a skill_name field
skill_name matches the target skill name
evals.json has an evals array
evals array has at least 3 eval cases (minimum for reliable signal)
First eval case has an id field
First eval case has a prompt field
First eval case has an expectations field
First eval prompt is non-trivially long (at least 30 chars)

deterministic_checks.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('evals.json')); evals=d.get('evals',[]); det_count=sum(1 for e in evals if e.get('deterministic_checks',[])); print(f'{det_count}/{len(evals)} evals have deterministic_checks'); exit(0 if det_count>0 else 1)"
deterministic_checks.description: At least one eval case has deterministic_checks defined

deterministic_checks.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('evals.json')); evals=d.get('evals',[]); all_ok=all(all(k in e for k in ['id','prompt','expectations']) for e in evals); print('All evals have required fields' if all_ok else 'Missing fields'); exit(0 if all_ok else 1)"
deterministic_checks.description: Every eval case has required fields: id, prompt, expectations

expectations: Detects eval-doctor mode from the user input
Reads the sample skill's SKILL.md to understand what it does
Generated eval prompts are realistic and specific to text formatting, not generic placeholders
Each eval case has expectations that are discriminating (would fail for wrong output)
Suggests running the full improvement loop after evals are ready

evals.prompt: Show me the autoresearch convergence report for the workspace at tests/fixtures/sample-skill-autoresearch/
evals.expected_output: Reads results.tsv and grading files, produces a convergence report with score trajectory table and recommendation

deterministic_checks.description: Convergence report file was created
Report contains the baseline score 0.45 from results.tsv
Report contains the iteration 1 score 0.67 from results.tsv
Report contains the iteration 2 score 0.50 from results.tsv
Report mentions that iteration 1 was kept or improved
Report mentions that iteration 2 was reverted or discarded
Report shows all three iterations (0, 1, 2) in order
Report references v1 as the best version
Report contains a recommendation or next-steps section

expectations: Detects report mode from the user input
Reads results.tsv from the workspace directory
Displays a score trajectory showing all three iterations with their scores
Shows that iteration 1 was kept (score improved from 0.45 to 0.67) and iteration 2 was reverted (score dropped to 0.50)
Includes a recommendation about whether to apply changes or continue iterating
